heading: Capacity for nursing students and faculty
label: 1161d Capacity for nursing students and faculty
notes: Amendments2009—Subsec. (d). substituted “Definitions” for “Definition” in heading, added par. (2), and struck out former par. (2). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The terms ‘accredited school of nursing’ and ‘accredited nursing program’ have the meaning given those terms in .”Pub. L. 111–39section 296 of title 42

children.text: an accredited registered nursing program at the baccalaureate or associate degree level to enable such program to expand the faculty and facilities of such program to accommodate additional students in such program; or
an accredited graduate-level nursing program to accommodate advanced practice degrees for registered nurses or to accommodate students enrolled in such program to become teachers of nursing students.

children.heading: Determination of number of students and application
children.label: b Determination of number of students and application

children.text: determine, for the four academic years preceding the academic year for which the determination is made, the average number of matriculated nursing program students, in each of the institution’s accredited associate, baccalaureate, or advanced nursing degree programs at such institution for such academic years;

submit an application to the Secretary at such time, in such manner, and accompanied by such information as the Secretary may require, including the average number in each of the institution’s accredited nursing programs determined under paragraph (1); and

children.label: i
children.text: be registered nurses in pursuit of a master’s or doctoral degree in nursing; and

children.label: ii
children.text: have a contractual obligation with the hospital or health facility that is in partnership with the institution of higher education;

children.label: B
children.text: the hospital or health facility of employment will be the clinical site for the accredited school of nursing program, if the program requires a clinical site;

children.label: i
children.text: maintain their employment on at least a part-time basis with the hospital or health facility that allowed them to participate in the program; and

children.label: ii
children.text: receive an income from the hospital or health facility, as at least a part-time employee, and release times or flexible schedules, to accommodate their program requirements, as necessary; and

children.label: D
children.text: upon completion of the program, recipients of scholarships described in subsection (c)(2)(B)(ii)(III) will be required to teach for two years in an accredited school of nursing for each year of support the individual received under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: the number of matriculated nursing program students at such institution for such academic year, exceeds

children.label: B
children.text: the average number determined with respect to such institution under subsection (b)(1).

children.heading: Distribution of grants among different degree programs
children.label: 2 Distribution of grants among different degree programs
i
children.text: use 20 percent of such funds to award grants under this section to institutions of higher education for the purpose of accommodating advanced practice degrees or students in accredited graduate-level nursing programs;

children.label: ii
children.text: use 40 percent of such funds to award grants under this section to institutions of higher education for the purpose of expanding accredited registered nurse programs at the baccalaureate degree level; and

children.label: iii
children.text: use 40 percent of such funds to award grants under this section to institutions of higher education for the purpose of expanding accredited registered nurse programs at the associate degree level.

children.label: I
children.text: the purchase of distance learning technologies and expanding methods of delivery of instruction to include alternatives to onsite learning; and

children.label: II
children.text: the collection, analysis, and dissemination of data on educational outcomes and best practices identified through the activities described in this section; and

children.label: I
children.text: funding release time for qualified nurses enrolled in the graduate nursing program;

children.label: II
children.text: providing for faculty salaries; or

children.label: III
children.text: providing scholarships to qualified nurses in pursuit of an advanced degree with the goal of becoming faculty members in an accredited nursing program.

children.heading: Geographic distribution
children.label: i Geographic distribution
children.text: Providing an equitable geographic distribution of such grants.

children.heading: Urban and rural areas
children.label: ii Urban and rural areas
children.text: Distributing such grants to urban and rural areas.

children.heading: Range and type of institution
children.label: iii Range and type of institution
children.text: Ensuring that the activities to be assisted are developed for a range of types and sizes of institutions of higher education, including institutions providing alternative methods of delivery of instruction in addition to on-site learning.

children.heading: Distribution of excess funds
children.label: D Distribution of excess funds
children.text: If, for a fiscal year, funds described in clause (i), (ii), or (iii) of subparagraph (A) remain available after the Secretary awards grants under this section to all applicants for the particular category of accredited nursing programs described in such clause, the Secretary shall use equal amounts of the remaining funds to award grants under this section to applicants that applied under the other categories of nursing programs.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: E Limitation
children.text: Of the amount appropriated to carry out this section, the Secretary may award not more than ten percent of such amount for the optional purposes under subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Health facility
children.label: 1 Health facility
children.text: The term “health facility” means an Indian health service center, a Native Hawaiian health center, a hospital, a federally qualified health center, a rural health clinic, a nursing home, a home health agency, a hospice program, a public health clinic, a State or local department of public health, a skilled nursing facility, or an ambulatory surgical center.

children.heading: Public Health Service Act
children.label: 2 Public Health Service Act
children.text: The terms “accredited” and “school of nursing” have the meanings given those terms in section 801 of the Public Health Service Act ().42 U.S.C. 296

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Funds provided under this section may not be used for the construction of new facilities.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 2 Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in paragraph (1) shall be construed to prohibit funds provided under this section from being used for the repair or renovation of facilities.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: f Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section such sums as may be necessary for fiscal year 2009 and each of the five succeeding fiscal years.
